description_long: This research institute consists of several interconnected rectangular and gabled structures, with a prominent section featuring a flat roof covered in blue solar panels. The building is set on a black base platform, with surrounding areas of green and pink suggesting landscaping and paved walkways. The main structure is constructed from light gray materials, while the adjacent gabled-roof section is clad in corrugated metal. There are multiple entry points, including a sheltered area beneath the solar panels and a more traditional entrance under the gabled roof. Utility poles and street elements are present, indicating integration with urban infrastructure. The overall design is functional and modern, with an emphasis on sustainability and accessibility.
description: A modern research institute with flat and gabled roofs, blue solar panels, and a mix of gray and pink exterior surfaces, set on a black platform with landscaped surroundings.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the gabled-roof section in the foreground, the main flat-roofed building with solar panels, and a glimpse of the entryway and adjacent structures.
description_view_1: View 1 highlights the flat-roofed section with solar panels and the black platform, with the gabled section partially visible to the left.
description_view_2: View 2 presents the building from the street side, emphasizing the solar panel array and the sheltered area beneath, with the gabled roof receding into the background.
description_view_3: View 3 focuses on the rear and side of the building, showing the pink and gray surfaces, the gabled roof, and the integration with the black base and landscaping.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is located beneath the overhang of the flat-roofed section, featuring a wide, accessible opening with no visible steps, likely accommodating double doors. The area is sheltered by the building's overhang and is adjacent to paved walkways, providing easy access for all users. No decorative elements or signage are visible.
